issue.title: Scale dep graph node height by plan phase count — small fixes look small, epics look big

successCriteria.description: useDependencyGraph fetches plan files in parallel with issue bodies and surfaces phase count per issue
Node visual height varies with plan phase count: 1 phase → 50px, 2-3 → 60px, 4-6 → 75px, 7+ → 90px
Dagre layout reserves matching vertical space so variable heights don't cause overlap or excessive gaps
Issues with no plan file render at the default height (60px) without errors
Swimlane bands from #149 still rank-group correctly — heights vary but x-positions stay aligned per rank
Hover lineage from #149 still works — height is orthogonal to lineage tracing
Progress bar from #149 still anchors to the bottom edge of variable-height nodes (position: absolute)

phases.title: Wire plan fetch + apply variable node heights
verification: Read post-edit useDependencyGraph.ts: confirm computeNodeHeight helper exists, Promise.all invokes get_plan alongside fetch_github_issue_by_number, layoutGraph signature takes heights map and uses pos.y - h/2 for y-centering
Read post-edit IssueNode.tsx: confirm IssueNodeData has phaseCount, root div has inline minHeight style
Run `cargo check` from apps/desktop/src-tauri — must be clean (sanity check, no Rust changes expected)

Open Dependency Graph for v0.5.2 in the desktop app: confirm #152 (1 phase) renders shorter than a multi-phase issue from history; if v0.5.1 issues have plans, confirm their nodes are visibly distinct sizes
Open Dependency Graph for a release where no plans exist (e.g., very old archived release): confirm all nodes render at default 60px without errors in devtools console
Hover any node on a release with edges: confirm lineage dimming/highlighting from #149 still works at any node height
Confirm progress bar (#149 Phase 2) stays anchored to bottom edge on the tallest (90px) nodes — should not float in middle
